environment.notes: Loaded the integration artifact via file:// in real headless Chromium with real network. No production runtime dependency was stubbed. USGS feed returned HTTP 200; OSM tile pyramid loaded for zoom levels 2 and 3 across the pan/zoom interactions.

assumption_checks.evidence: Single web page artifact: index.html + co-located JS/CSS. No report/dataset/pipeline output.
Map region occupies 91.875% of the 1280x800 viewport per measured bounding rect. Earthquakes rendered as L.circleMarker SVG paths (480 markers in past-24h feed run). No heatmap/polygon overlays.

magnitudeRadius and magnitudeColor encode magnitude on size and color. Live exercise observed 4 distinct fill colors across rendered markers (#ffffb2, #fed976, #fd8d3c, #f03b20); the 5th bucket #bd0026 is M>=6 and was not present in this 24h sample but is reachable per code path.
No login/auth UI in DOM; no auth/session code in source.
No localStorage/sessionStorage/IndexedDB/cookie writes for user state in any source file.
No file picker / drag-drop / URL parameter for data; data-fetcher.js fetches the fixed USGS public GeoJSON URL.

Initial map view set to center [20,0], zoom 2 (worldCopyJump enabled). Live exercise: tile pyramid at z=2 loaded a global view; markers rendered across multiple continents (480 across the past-24h feed).

Pan via mouse drag shifted .leaflet-map-pane transform from translate3d(0,0,0) to translate3d(283px,113px,0); SVG viewBox shifted accordingly. Zoom-in control click loaded new z=3 tiles. Marker click opened popup containing time, magnitude, place, depth.
Viewport meta width=1280; styles use desktop-typical proportions; no horizontal scroll at 1280x800 baseline; no mobile hamburger nav.
Artifact opens directly via file:// without any backend; Leaflet loaded from vendor/leaflet/ paths; no server code present.
No focal-mechanism/depth-slice/fault-overlay specialized seismologist tooling. Single map + legend + popup audience-appropriate UI.

out_of_scope_checks.evidence: No login form, no auth code paths, no Authorization headers in fetch.
No save/bookmark/annotate UI; no persistence calls in source.
No share-link / collaborator / multiplayer UI in DOM.
Desktop-baseline layout; no mobile-first @media query primitives; viewport pinned at width=1280.
Static file artifact; no server, DB client, or own API endpoints.
No file inputs / drag-drop in DOM (input[type=file] count = 0).
No forecast/predict/risk text or code paths in the source.
No such overlays referenced in map.js; no plugin imports.
No Notification API usage; no subscribe UI.
No export/download buttons in DOM; no Blob/anchor[download] code.
Reasonable defaults only (aria-label on map, role=alert on banner). No formal-compliance claim.
Single-language English strings; no i18n library; no locale switcher.

inflection_point_checks.evidence: data-fetcher.js: single await fetch with no setInterval/setTimeout/recursion. main.js: bootstrap invoked once on DOMContentLoaded. Live exercise: marker count stable at 480 before and after 4-second observation window (no auto-refresh side effects).
FEED_URL targets all_day.geojson. DOM has 0 input[type=range], 0 date inputs, 0 select elements. No window-control UI.
No magnitude/depth/region threshold inputs in DOM. data-fetcher.js maps every feature; map.js iterates all events (skips only entries with non-finite lat/lon — safety guard, not a filter).

td_inflection_point_checks.evidence: index.html loads vendor/leaflet/leaflet.{js,css}; map.js calls L.map(), L.tileLayer(), L.circleMarker(); no Mapbox/MapLibre/CDN references anywhere.
USGS_FEED_URL constant matches the contracted endpoint; live exercise returned HTTP 200 from this endpoint and produced 480 normalized records.
magnitudeRadius monotonic (3 + 1.6*max(0,m), clamped to 22). magnitudeColor returns 5 distinct hex values across [-1,9]. Legend renders all 5 buckets with size+color swatches in the rendered DOM.

artifact_exercise_results.scenario: Open output/integration/index.html via file:// in headless Chromium with real network connectivity. Wait for the page to settle.
artifact_exercise_results.expected: A geographic map fills a substantial portion of the viewport. Multiple earthquake markers are visible on it, magnitude-encoded. Pan, zoom, and marker-click->popup work.
artifact_exercise_results.actual: Map filled 91.875% of the 1280x800 viewport (>= 50% threshold). 480 earthquake markers rendered as SVG circle paths in .leaflet-overlay-pane, with 4 distinct fill colors observed across the magnitude range. Pan via mouse drag shifted the map pane transform and SVG viewBox. Zoom-in control click triggered loading of z=3 OSM tiles. Marker click opened a Leaflet popup showing place ('9 km ENE of Ocotillo Wells, CA'), magnitude (1.5), depth (4.5 km), and human-readable time. No page errors, no console errors.

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: displays earthquake events as markers on a geographic map
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: Loaded artifact in real Chromium; .leaflet-container present, .leaflet-tile-pane has 24 tile imgs (geographic basemap), 480 .leaflet-overlay-pane svg path nodes (event markers).

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: see where earthquakes have occurred and get a sense of distribution and intensity at a glance
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: 480 markers distributed across the global basemap (worldCopyJump + initial center [20,0] z=2 view). Marker fill colors observed in 4 distinct buckets (#ffffb2 -> #fed976 -> #fd8d3c -> #f03b20) and radii vary by magnitude (legend confirms: M<2, 2-4, 4-5, 5-6, 6+ rendered with size+color swatches). Intensity legible without interaction.

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: Map is the primary UI surface; earthquakes are point markers
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: Map region: x=0, y=65, width=1280, height=735; area = 91.875% of viewport. Markers are L.circleMarker SVG paths (point markers, not heatmap, not polygons).

Initial view at center [20,0] zoom 2 with worldCopyJump=true shows the whole world. Live feed produced 480 markers spanning multiple continents (sample popup hit a California event; feed worldwide-by-default).
Viewport meta width=1280; rendered at 1280x800 with no horizontal scroll, no mobile hamburger nav, header/legend/map laid out for desktop proportions.

Page loaded directly from file:// URL; vendor/leaflet/leaflet.{js,css} resolved as relative paths; no backend/server required. Leaflet global L present, EarthquakeMap & EarthquakeData modules registered correctly.

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: produces a visible, interactive (pan, zoom) world map
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: Map canvas visible (.leaflet-container present, 91.875% viewport coverage). Pan: mouse drag from center shifted .leaflet-map-pane transform from translate3d(0,0,0) to translate3d(283px,113px,0); SVG viewBox shifted from '-128 -73 1536 882' to '-411 -186 1536 882'. Zoom: clicking .leaflet-control-zoom-in triggered tile pyramid request at z=3 (was z=2).

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: Clicking a marker opens a popup with time, magnitude, place, depth
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: Click on first marker opened .leaflet-popup. Popup content: '9 km ENE of Ocotillo Wells, CA / Magnitude: 1.5 / Depth: 4.5 km / Time: 5/9/2026, 10:14:46 AM' — all four required fields present, time is human-readable (toLocaleString).
Exactly 1 <header> element present, containing non-empty title 'Earthquake Activity — Past 24 Hours' and subtitle 'Worldwide seismic events from the USGS public feed.'

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: map region occupies dominant area of viewport
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: At 1280x800 baseline, #map bounding rect = (0, 65, 1280, 735) = 91.875% of viewport area. Threshold >= 50% met.

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: visible legend labels at least three magnitude buckets
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: #legend visible. legend-list contains 5 .legend__item entries: 'M < 2', 'M 2 - 4', 'M 4 - 5', 'M 5 - 6', 'M 6+'. Each item has a circular size+color swatch styled via shared EarthquakeMap.magnitudeColor / magnitudeRadius.

artifact_exercise_results.phrase: page sized for typical desktop viewport
artifact_exercise_results.evidence: Rendered at 1280x800: header + map + legend overlay; no horizontal scroll; no mobile-only navigation; layout proportions match desktop baseline.

prompt_named_verb_result.scenario: Open output/integration/index.html via file:// in real headless Chromium with allowed network access, viewport 1280x800.
prompt_named_verb_result.environment: Playwright (chromium, headless build 147.0.7727.15) — production fidelity: vendored Leaflet loaded, real fetch to USGS, real OSM tile requests. No runtime dependency stubbed.
prompt_named_verb_result.expected: A visible map fills the viewport; multiple earthquake markers appear, magnitude-encoded; pan, zoom, and marker-click-to-popup work as a user would expect.
prompt_named_verb_result.actual: Map covers 91.875% of viewport. 480 markers rendered with 4 distinct color buckets observed (5 defined). Pan and zoom both interactive (verified via map-pane transform mutation and tile-pyramid zoom-level change). Marker click opens popup with place/magnitude/depth/human-readable-time. No page or console errors.

cv_resolution.IP1.A2: pass (marker count stable at 480 across 4-second observation; no refresh code path exists by source inspection)

edge_case_testing_cross_check.notes: Builder-4a's report explicitly deferred all real-browser user-flow assertions to CV with the rationale that this run permits static + structural inspection in section-4. Static prediction was 'pass'. CV's production-fidelity run confirms each deferred assertion passes.

key_observations: Production-fidelity exercise used real Chromium loading file:// with vendored Leaflet, real USGS fetch (HTTP 200), and real OSM tile loading. No stubs.
USGS past-24h feed delivered 480 events. All rendered as L.circleMarker SVG paths.

Live distinct fill colors observed: 4 of 5 defined buckets. The fifth (deep red #bd0026, M>=6) was not present in this 24h sample but the code path is verifiably correct via static inspection of magnitudeColor.
Edge-case-testing builder ran static-only and explicitly deferred runtime user-flow assertions to CV; CV resolved all of them as pass.
